basic_details.job_title: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Recruitment 2026: 681 Vacancies, Apply Online
basic_details.department: Technical Education and Industrial Training Department, Punjab
basic_details.short_information: The Subordinate Services Selection Board, Punjab (SSSB Punjab) has released Advertisement No. 03 of 2026 inviting online applications for 681 Craft Instructor posts under the Technical Education and Industrial Training Department (Industrial Training Wing), Government of Punjab. The posts span 52 different trades, divided between Degree/Diploma qualification holders and NTC/NAC certificate holders. Online applications can be submitted from 10 July 2026 to 30 July 2026.

application_fee.details: Note: Fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances. No fee waiver or concession will be granted to any candidate.

age_limit.details: Age is reckoned as on 01-01-2026. | Note: State/Central Government employees seeking upper age relaxation must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their department along with their application.

qualification.details: All 52 trades require, at minimum, 10th pass with Science and Mathematics under the 10+2 system from a recognized School or Board. Beyond this common academic requirement, the technical qualification follows one of two pathways depending on which annexure the trade falls under: | A relevant Bachelor's Degree/B.Voc in the corresponding engineering discipline from an AICTE/UGC-recognized institution, plus one year of relevant industry or teaching experience; OR A 3-year Diploma in the corresponding discipline from an AICTE-recognized institution or relevant Advanced Diploma (Vocational) from the Director General of Training, plus two years of relevant industry or teaching experience. The exact engineering/technology discipline required (e.g., Mechanical, Civil, Electronics, Instrumentation, Chemical, Textile, Agriculture Engineering, etc.) varies by trade - see the full notification for trade-specific requirements. Degree/Diploma holders selected under this category must acquire the National Craft Instructor Certificate (NCIC) in their respective trade within three years of appointment, failing which their service will be terminated. | A National Trade Certificate (NTC) or National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in the corresponding trade from a recognized institution; A National Craft Instructor Certificate (NCIC) in the corresponding trade; and Three years of relevant experience in an organization registered under the Factories Act, 1948, or teaching experience in a government or government-recognized institution. | Punjabi Language: As per Rule 17 of the Punjab Civil Services (General and Common Conditions of Service) Rules, 1994, candidates must have passed Matriculation with Punjabi as a compulsory or elective subject, or an equivalent Punjabi language examination. Cut-off date for qualifications: All required educational and technical qualifications must be completed on or before the last date of application submission (30-07-2026). Departmental (Practical) Examination: After appointment, Craft Instructors must undergo practical industry training and pass a practical examination conducted by the Government, within three attempts over three years from appointment. Failure may result in termination or reversion to a prior post.

salary_details.details: Selected Craft Instructors (across all trades listed in Annexure-A and Annexure-B) will be placed on Rs. 35,400/- (Level-6) as per the 7th Central Pay Commission scale, subject to applicable allowances as notified by the Finance Department, Government of Punjab.

selection_process: Candidates who successfully apply will be called for an Objective Type (Multiple Choice Question) written examination. The merit list is prepared based on marks obtained in the written exam. Each correct answer carries 1 mark; each wrong answer attracts a negative marking of ¼ mark. In case of a tie in merit
preference is given first to the candidate with the older date of birth. If still tied
preference goes to the candidate with a higher percentage in the required educational qualification. If still tied
preference goes to the candidate with higher marks in Matriculation. Based on the merit list

candidates are called for counseling and document/certificate verification. The number of candidates called is decided by the Board based on category-wise vacancy count. Being shortlisted for the exam

or being called for counseling does not itself confer any right to selection - final recommendation depends on the candidate proving their eligibility in all respects. Candidates in the Sports category will have their merit determined as per Punjab Sports Department notification dated 09-03-2026 and subsequent instructions.
how_to_apply: Visit the Board's website at sssb.punjab.gov.in and click the "Apply Online" link between 10-07-2026 and 30-07-2026. Applications through any other method will not be accepted.
New candidates (without an existing login) click "Registration" and fill in their details to generate a User ID and Password. The User ID will be the email ID used during registration.
After successful registration, log in using the registered Email ID and password to proceed with the application.
Fill in personal, educational, and other required details completely and accurately.
Upload a passport-sized photograph (or capture live via camera) and a scanned copy of your signature.
Click "Save Draft" or "Next," then click "Attach Annexure" to upload your 10th class certificate, educational qualification certificates, and other mandatory documents.
Click "Save Documents," then proceed to "Make Payment" to pay the online application fee.
After successful fee payment, click "Submit" to complete the application. The full application form will be displayed on screen.
Take a printout of the submitted application form and retain it for future reference.
Note: A saved but incomplete draft application can be resumed via "Menu > View Status of Application > View Incomplete Application."

Important: Once submitted, no correction is possible under any circumstances. Candidates are advised to fill the form themselves rather than through a cyber café or third party, since no correction request will be entertained after submission. If the online fee transaction fails for any reason, the application is treated as not submitted, and the fee already paid is non-refundable.

content_sections.heading: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The 681 posts are classified trade-wise and category-wise into two annexures: Annexure-A for Degree/Diploma qualified candidates and Annexure-B for NTC/NAC (National Trade Certificate/National Apprenticeship Certificate) qualified candidates. Attendant Operator (Chemical Plant), Draughtsman Civil, Electronics Mechanic, Electroplater, Fitter, Industrial Painter, Instrument Mechanic, Machinist, Machinist Grinder, Mechanic Agricultural Machinery, Mechanic Auto Electrical and Electronics, Mechanic Diesel Engine, Mechanic Motor Vehicle, Mechanic Tractor, Painter (General), Plastic Processing Operator, Plumber, Refrigeration and Air Conditioner Technician, Spinning Technician, Surveyor, Tool & Die Maker (Press Tools, Jigs & Fixtures), Turner, Welder, Wood Work Technician, and Workshop Calculation & Science. Attendant Operator (Chemical Plant), Computer Hardware and Network Maintenance, Draughtsman Civil, Electrician, Electronics Mechanic, Electroplater, Fitter, Industrial Painter, Information and Communication Technology System Maintenance, Machinist, Machinist Grinder, Mechanic Agricultural Machinery, Mechanic Tractor, Mechanic Auto Electrical and Electronics, Mechanic Consumer Electronics Appliances, Mechanic Diesel Engine, Painter (General), Plastic Processing Operator, Plumber, Refrigeration and Air Conditioner Technician, Spinning Technician, Surveyor, Tool & Die Maker (Press Tools, Jigs & Fixtures), Turner, Welder, Welder (GMAW & GTAW), and Wood Work Technician. Note: A detailed category-wise breakup (General, SC, BC, EWS, Ex-Serviceman, Sports, PwD, Freedom Fighter) for each individual trade is provided in Annexure-A and Annexure-B of the official notification. Candidates should refer to the official PDF for the exact reserved-category count applicable to their specific trade before applying.

content_sections.heading: Category Codes for Applying
content_sections.text: Note: Category code 118 and 120 do not appear in the official notification's list; this is as published in the source document.

content_sections.heading: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Educational and Technical Qualification
content_sections.text: All 52 trades require, at minimum, 10th pass with Science and Mathematics under the 10+2 system from a recognized School or Board. Beyond this common academic requirement, the technical qualification follows one of two pathways depending on which annexure the trade falls under: A relevant Bachelor's Degree/B.Voc in the corresponding engineering discipline from an AICTE/UGC-recognized institution, plus one year of relevant industry or teaching experience; OR A 3-year Diploma in the corresponding discipline from an AICTE-recognized institution or relevant Advanced Diploma (Vocational) from the Director General of Training, plus two years of relevant industry or teaching experience. The exact engineering/technology discipline required (e.g., Mechanical, Civil, Electronics, Instrumentation, Chemical, Textile, Agriculture Engineering, etc.) varies by trade - see the full notification for trade-specific requirements. Degree/Diploma holders selected under this category must acquire the National Craft Instructor Certificate (NCIC) in their respective trade within three years of appointment, failing which their service will be terminated. A National Trade Certificate (NTC) or National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in the corresponding trade from a recognized institution; A National Craft Instructor Certificate (NCIC) in the corresponding trade; and Three years of relevant experience in an organization registered under the Factories Act, 1948, or teaching experience in a government or government-recognized institution. Punjabi Language: As per Rule 17 of the Punjab Civil Services (General and Common Conditions of Service) Rules, 1994, candidates must have passed Matriculation with Punjabi as a compulsory or elective subject, or an equivalent Punjabi language examination. Cut-off date for qualifications: All required educational and technical qualifications must be completed on or before the last date of application submission (30-07-2026). Departmental (Practical) Examination: After appointment, Craft Instructors must undergo practical industry training and pass a practical examination conducted by the Government, within three attempts over three years from appointment. Failure may result in termination or reversion to a prior post.

content_sections.heading: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Age Limit
content_sections.text: Age is reckoned as on 01-01-2026 . Note: State/Central Government employees seeking upper age relaxation must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their department along with their application.

rows.category: SC and BC candidates domiciled in Punjab

rows.age_limit: As per Punjab Recruitment of Ex-Servicemen Rules, 1982 (service period deducted from actual age; relaxed age must not exceed the post's upper age limit by more than 3 years)

rows.category: Widows, divorcees, and certain other categories of married women

content_sections.heading: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Salary
content_sections.text: Selected Craft Instructors (across all trades listed in Annexure-A and Annexure-B) will be placed on Rs. 35,400/- (Level-6) as per the 7th Central Pay Commission scale, subject to applicable allowances as notified by the Finance Department, Government of Punjab.

content_sections.heading: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Application Fee
content_sections.text: Note: Fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances. No fee waiver or concession will be granted to any candidate.

rows.category: Ex-Serviceman, Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), Persons with Disabilities (PwD), and Lineal Descendants of Ex-Serviceman (LDESM) of Punjab State only
Scheduled Castes and Backward Classes of Punjab State only

content_sections.heading: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: Reservation benefits are available only to candidates domiciled in Punjab State. A Domicile/Residence Certificate is mandatory for every candidate (regardless of category) and must not be older than 5 years at the time of upload/submission. SC/BC/Ex-Serviceman/EWS certificates must conform to prevailing Government of Punjab instructions. Reservation for women is governed by the Punjab Civil Services (Reservation of Posts for Women) Rules, 2020, and subsequent amendments. Ex-Serviceman category posts will first be filled by Ex-Serviceman (Self) candidates; only if unavailable will they be filled by Ex-Serviceman (Dependent) candidates, per Government of Punjab notification dated 08-05-2020.

content_sections.heading: SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Selection Process
content_sections.text: Candidates who successfully apply will be called for an Objective Type (Multiple Choice Question) written examination . The merit list is prepared based on marks obtained in the written exam. Each correct answer carries 1 mark; each wrong answer attracts a negative marking of ¼ mark. In case of a tie in merit, preference is given first to the candidate with the older date of birth. If still tied, preference goes to the candidate with a higher percentage in the required educational qualification. If still tied, preference goes to the candidate with higher marks in Matriculation. Based on the merit list, candidates are called for counseling and document/certificate verification. The number of candidates called is decided by the Board based on category-wise vacancy count. Being shortlisted for the exam, appearing for it, or being called for counseling does not itself confer any right to selection - final recommendation depends on the candidate proving their eligibility in all respects. Candidates in the Sports category will have their merit determined as per Punjab Sports Department notification dated 09-03-2026 and subsequent instructions.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Passport-sized photograph (uploaded or captured live via camera) Scanned signature 10th class certificate Certificates related to the required educational/technical qualification Domicile/Residence Certificate of Punjab (not older than 5 years) SC/BC/EWS/Ex-Serviceman/PwD certificate, where applicable, as per prevailing Punjab Government format Other mandatory documents as specified on the application portal Note: Applications submitted without photograph and signature will be treated as incomplete and rejected.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the Board's website at sssb.punjab.gov.in and click the "Apply Online" link between 10-07-2026 and 30-07-2026. Applications through any other method will not be accepted. New candidates (without an existing login) click "Registration" and fill in their details to generate a User ID and Password. The User ID will be the email ID used during registration. After successful registration, log in using the registered Email ID and password to proceed with the application. Fill in personal, educational, and other required details completely and accurately. Upload a passport-sized photograph (or capture live via camera) and a scanned copy of your signature. Click "Save Draft" or "Next," then click "Attach Annexure" to upload your 10th class certificate, educational qualification certificates, and other mandatory documents. Click "Save Documents," then proceed to "Make Payment" to pay the online application fee. After successful fee payment, click "Submit" to complete the application. The full application form will be displayed on screen. Take a printout of the submitted application form and retain it for future reference. Note: A saved but incomplete draft application can be resumed via "Menu > View Status of Application > View Incomplete Application." Important: Once submitted, no correction is possible under any circumstances. Candidates are advised to fill the form themselves rather than through a cyber café or third party, since no correction request will be entertained after submission. If the online fee transaction fails for any reason, the application is treated as not submitted, and the fee already paid is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q1. How many Craft Instructor vacancies has PSSSB announced for 2026?
faq.answer: PSSSB/SSSB Punjab has announced 681 Craft Instructor vacancies (various trades) under Advertisement No. 03/2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for SSSB Punjab Craft Instructor recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application and fee payment must be completed by 30 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. How many total vacancies are there in Advertisement No. 03 of 2026?
faq.answer: There are 681 Craft Instructor posts across 52 trades, split between Annexure-A (Degree/Diploma, 250 posts) and Annexure-B (NTC/NAC, 431 posts).

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary of a Craft Instructor in Punjab?
faq.answer: Rs. 35,400/- (Level-6) as per the 7th Central Pay Commission scale.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: 18 to 37 years as on 01-01-2026 for General category, with relaxation up to 42 years for Punjab-domiciled SC/BC candidates, and further relaxations for government employees and ex-servicemen.

faq.question: Q6. Is knowledge of Punjabi mandatory?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates must have passed Matriculation with Punjabi as a compulsory or elective subject, or an equivalent examination.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process?
faq.answer: An Objective Type (MCQ) written exam with negative marking of ¼ mark per wrong answer, followed by a merit list and document verification/counseling.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee?
faq.answer: Rs. 500 for Ex-Serviceman/EWS/PwD/LDESM, Rs. 750 for SC/BC of Punjab, and Rs. 1,500 for all other categories.

faq.question: Q9. Can reservation benefits be claimed by non-Punjab residents?
faq.answer: No. Reservation benefits are available only to candidates domiciled in Punjab State.
